ddls:
CREATE TABLE "access_points" (
  "ap_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access point */,
  "ap_name" TEXT /* Name of the access point */,
  "ap_description" TEXT /* Description of the access point */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the access point */,
  "installed_date" TEXT /* Date when the access point was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance of the access point */,
  "hardware_version" TEXT /* Version of the hardware */,
  "software_version" TEXT /* Version of the software */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ap_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "log_entries" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "ap_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the access point that generated the log entry */,
  "log_header" TEXT /* Log header information */,
  "log_date" TEXT /* Date when the log entry was generated */,
  "wifi_ap_state" TEXT /* Wi-Fi AP state (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "bssid" TEXT /* Basic Service Set ID used for this AP */,
  "state" TEXT /* State of the Wi-Fi AP (see Table 160, Wi-Fi AP States) */,
  "num_stations" INTEGER /* Number of clients connected to the AP */,
  "log_type" TEXT /* Type of log (e.g., security, performance, configuration) */,
  "log_details" TEXT /* Detailed information about the log entry */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the log entry (e.g., info, warning, error) */,
  "source_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the source generating the log */,
  "destination_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the destination (if applicable) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Exact time when the log entry was generated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_log_entries_ap_id FOREIGN KEY ("ap_id") REFERENCES access_points ("ap_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, analyst) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, inactive, locked) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "log_id" INTEGER /* ID of the log entry being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the log entry */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the log entry was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the user accessed the log entry */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string for the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_log_id FOREIGN KEY ("log_id") REFERENCES log_entries ("log_id")
)
